metadata.title: Weather Basics
metadata.source_description: Supplemental A1 vocab selected from coverage gaps

examples.english: The rain will stop soon.

item.notes: Coverage gap: weather. Very useful verb for talking about changing weather conditions.
item.image_prompt: Polished digital illustration of rain ending over a Korean street, clouds opening and drops slowing, umbrellas lowering, no text.

image.prompt: Polished digital illustration of rain ending over a Korean street, clouds opening and drops slowing, umbrellas lowering, no text. Stylish, contemporary editorial illustration for an adult language learner in their 30s. Keep it warm, playful, and visually engaging without looking childish. Use clean composition, rich color, and a polished modern textbook feel. If the scene includes people, depict Korean people in a natural contemporary Korean setting. No text in the image.

cards.back_html:
stop
그치다
geu-chi-da

Examples

  • 비가 곧 그쳐요.
    The rain will stop soon.
Coverage gap: weather. Very useful verb for talking about changing weather conditions.
Source: New vocab proposal • coverage-gap • weather

그치다
stop
geu-chi-da

Examples

  • 비가 곧 그쳐요.
    The rain will stop soon.
Coverage gap: weather. Very useful verb for talking about changing weather conditions.
Source: New vocab proposal • coverage-gap • weather

item.image_prompt: Polished digital illustration of a Korean road freezing in winter, shiny icy surface, cold air, nearby cars and bare trees, no text.

image.prompt: Polished digital illustration of a Korean road freezing in winter, shiny icy surface, cold air, nearby cars and bare trees, no text. Stylish, contemporary editorial illustration for an adult language learner in their 30s. Keep it warm, playful, and visually engaging without looking childish. Use clean composition, rich color, and a polished modern textbook feel. If the scene includes people, depict Korean people in a natural contemporary Korean setting. No text in the image.
